description_long: This is a large, round, earthenware bowl with a rough, matte surface and a reddish-brown coloration. The bowl is heavily damaged, with significant portions of the rim missing and visible cracks and chips throughout its body. The surface texture appears uneven, with some areas showing signs of weathering or age, and there are no visible decorative patterns or ornamentation. The interior and exterior both display a similar color and finish, suggesting it is made from a single type of clay or ceramic material. The bowl is hollow and open at the top, with a wide mouth and a deep cavity, making it suitable for holding or serving items. Its overall appearance suggests it may be an ancient or archaeological artifact, possibly used for utilitarian or ceremonial purposes.
description: A large, reddish-brown, heavily damaged earthenware bowl with a rough, matte surface and missing rim sections. The bowl is weathered and shows visible cracks and chips.
description_view_0: This view shows the bowl's jagged, broken rim and a large missing section at the front, highlighting the rough texture and reddish-brown color.
description_view_1: This angle reveals more of the bowl's side, with additional cracks and a slightly larger intact area of the rim, as well as some grayish discoloration.
description_view_2: The back view displays the most complete section of the rim, though cracks and chips are still visible, and the bowl's curvature is more apparent.
description_view_3: This side view shows a combination of broken and intact rim sections, with the bowl's deep cavity and rough, weathered surface clearly visible.
attribute_context_of_use: holding or serving food, or possibly for ritual or ceremonial use
